message:
fix(e2e): use repo-relative target dir for mmap scrollback test and sync beads

- Replace hardcoded /tmp/target-rch-ft-8vla with repo-relative
  target/rch-e2e-ft-8vla-<run_id> default to eliminate dep-info churn
  across rch compilation steps; still honors inherited CARGO_TARGET_DIR
- Fix unused-variable suppression in storage.rs test (remove _ prefix
  from rx binding that is actually used)
- Document the target dir strategy in mmap_scrollback_store.md
- Close bead ft-ic4nd tracking the vendored crate bug sweep

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-1memj.28.1","title":"[ft-1memj.28][support] Expand WebGPU helper edge-case tests","description":"Bounded support slice for ft-1memj.28. Expand existing non-GPU unit coverage in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/mod.rs around helper edge cases: surface format/view-format selection, dimension clamping, and WebGPU surface-error classification. Scope is test-only unless a genuine helper bug is uncovered. Heavy cargo validation, if run, must use rch.","notes":"Completed support slice: added WebGPU helper edge-case tests in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs covering first-family surface format selection, view-format normalization/deduplication, and large-dimension clamping. Validation used isolated rch worker vmi1152480 because most default workers lacked x11/cairo. During validation, fixed unrelated crates/frankenterm-gui/src/quad.rs test typing ambiguity that blocked the GUI test target. Passing remote command: cargo test -p frankenterm-gui webgpu::tests -- --nocapture.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkOriole","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:43:24.227479Z","created_by":"PinkOriole","updated_at":"2026-03-22T04:13:29Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T04:13:29Z","close_reason":"Completed: WebGPU helper edge-case tests added; remote rch webgpu::tests pass after quad.rs typing unblock.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","gui","rendering","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28.1","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.28","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:43:24.227479Z","created_by":"PinkOriole","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.29","title":"[gui][test] Mux layout and resize test suite","description":"# Mux layout and resize test suite

## What
Comprehensive tests for swap layouts, floating panes, constraint-based resize, CollapsePriority, and resize reflow scorecards.

## Why
These are the vendored mux/term modifications that differentiate FrankenTerm from stock WezTerm. They were developed but never tested in a real GUI context. Integration bugs are likely.

## Unit Tests

### Swap Layout Tests
- `test_swap_layout_create_named` — create layout with name, verify stored
- `test_swap_layout_cycle_forward` — cycle through 3 layouts, verify order
- `test_swap_layout_cycle_wraps` — cycling past last layout wraps to first
- `test_swap_layout_preserves_pane_content` — pane scrollback preserved across layout swap
- `test_swap_layout_pane_count_mismatch` — layout with 4 slots, 6 panes: extras handled gracefully
- `test_swap_layout_codec_roundtrip` — SwapToLayout PDU serializes/deserializes correctly
- `test_layout_cycle_codec_roundtrip` — SetLayoutCycle PDU roundtrip
- `test_layout_persistence` — layouts saved to disk, restored on restart

### Floating Pane Tests
- `test_floating_pane_create` — float a pane, verify it's in floating list
- `test_floating_pane_unfloat` — unfloat returns pane to tiled layout
- `test_floating_pane_z_order` — last-focused floating pane is on top
- `test_floating_pane_resize` — floating pane resized independently
- `test_floating_pane_move` — floating pane repositioned
- `test_floating_pane_overlap` — overlapping floats render correctly (z-order)
- `test_floating_pane_focus_cycle` — Tab cycles through floating panes

### CollapsePriority Tests
- `test_collapse_priority_default` — new panes have Normal priority
- `test_collapse_priority_set` — set priority to Low, verify
- `test_collapse_under_pressure` — with 10 panes and limited space, Low panes collapse first
- `test_collapse_never` — Never-priority panes never collapse
- `test_collapse_restore` — collapsed panes restore when space freed

### Constraint-Based Resize Tests
- `test_constraint_min_width` — pane won't shrink below min_width
- `test_constraint_max_width` — pane won't grow beyond max_width
- `test_constraint_codec_roundtrip` — UpdatePaneConstraints PDU roundtrip
- `test_constraint_conflict` — two panes with conflicting constraints: graceful resolution

### Resize Reflow Scorecard Tests
- `test_reflow_scorecard_clean_code` — code text reflows with low badness
- `test_reflow_scorecard_wide_table` — wide table reflows with high badness, gate triggers
- `test_reflow_gate_fallback` — when badness exceeds threshold, fallback strategy used
- `test_reflow_cursor_consistency` — cursor position preserved across resize (within tolerance)
- `test_reflow_viewport_priority` — viewport rows processed before scrollback

### E2E Script: `scripts/e2e_mux_layouts.sh`
```
1. Launch frankenterm-gui
2. Open 4 panes (2x2 grid)
3. Cycle layout to "tall" (verify arrangement changed)
4. Float pane #3 (verify floating overlay)
5. Resize window to 800x600 (verify constraints respected)
6. Resize to 400x300 (verify collapse behavior)
7. Restore to 1200x800 (verify collapsed panes restore)
8. Set pane #1 min_width=40 cols, resize window narrow, verify pane stays >= 40
9. Log all layout state transitions with timestamps
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All unit tests pass
- E2E script passes with all layout operations
- No panics or data loss during layout transitions
- Resize under constraint never produces panes with 0 width/height","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticDeer","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:37.561902Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T04:25:13.046955Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:20:20.064398Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gui","layouts","mux","resize","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:37.561902Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:54.209232Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:54.510795Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1702,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CORRECTION: There are 10 custom FrankenTerm codec PDUs (IDs 63-72), not 5. The full list: Floating pane PDUs (63-67): CreateFloatingPane, MoveFloatingPane, SetFloatingPaneZ, ToggleFloatingPane, RemoveFloatingPane. Layout PDUs (68-72): SwapToLayout, SetLayoutCycle, CycleStack, SelectStackPane, UpdatePaneConstraints. This test suite must cover roundtrip serialization/deserialization for ALL 10 PDUs, not just the 5 referenced in earlier descriptions.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:45:24Z"},{"id":1742,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by DustyFinch. Starting implementation of mux layout and resize test suite.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:02:03Z"},{"id":1744,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by BronzeFox. Starting focused first slice in frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs to expand stack/layout behavior tests (forward/backward + edge cases) before broadening to floating/collapse/constraints coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:03:14Z"},{"id":1745,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Slice update (BronzeFox): expanded mux stack cycling tests in frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs with edge cases: cycle_stack_backward_single_pane_stack_returns_none and cycle_stack_backward_invalid_slot_returns_none (alongside existing forward/backward roundtrip coverage). Validation attempted with rch exec: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/.cargo-bronzefox-check/target CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p mux cycle_stack_backward -- --nocapture. All remote rch workers currently unreachable; fail-open local run was terminated by external SIGTERM in build-script stage (proc-macro2), so no green test artifact yet.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:08:51Z"},{"id":1748,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed by DustyFinch. Test inventory: 256+ tests across layout.rs (25), tab.rs (80), codec/lib.rs (107), screen.rs (47). Added 3 new edge-case tests + e2e_mux_layouts.sh (9-step harness). Commit: 29fe155e.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:12:05Z"},{"id":1751,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CLOSED by RusticDeer. All acceptance criteria met. Commits: ef7c0fe5, b38ce2ee, 94ddc4fa.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:20:31Z"},{"id":1762,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.29","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Fresh-eyes bug fix (BronzeFox): re-applied stack-cycling slot-selection fix. GUI now cycles forward/backward using Tab::first_nontrivial_stack_slot_index() instead of hardcoded slot 0, preventing silent no-op when non-trivial stack lives in another slot. Added mux API + unit test first_nontrivial_stack_slot_index_identifies_cycleable_stack. Validation via rch exec: (1) cargo test -p mux -- --list confirms tests are registered; (2) cargo check -p frankenterm-gui --message-format=short completed successfully (warnings only).","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:25:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.3","title":"[gui][bootstrap] Minimal GUI binary that opens a terminal window","description":"# Minimal GUI binary that opens a terminal window

## What
Get `frankenterm-gui` to the point where `cargo run --bin frankenterm-gui` opens a working terminal window with a shell session.

## Why
This is the first milestone — proof that the vendored crate stack produces a functioning terminal. Everything else builds on this.

## How
1. Port `legacy_wezterm/wezterm-gui/src/main.rs` logic into `crates/frankenterm-gui/src/main.rs`
2. Adapt to use vendored crate paths (the modified mux/term/config, plus newly vendored window/font/client)
3. Wire up the config loader to prefer `frankenterm.toml` (using vendored `frankenterm/config/src/toml_config.rs`)
4. Set CFBundleExecutable, window title, and process name to "FrankenTerm"
5. Ensure basic operations work: type text, scrollback, copy/paste, resize, multiple tabs

## Key Behaviors to Verify
- Terminal launches with user's default shell
- Text input and output work
- Scrollback works
- Window resize reflows text (using the vendored term's reflow scorecard)
- Tabs work (Cmd+T to open, Cmd+W to close)
- Copy/paste works (Cmd+C/Cmd+V)
- Config is read from `~/.config/frankenterm/frankenterm.toml` if present

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo run --bin frankenterm-gui` opens a terminal window
- User can type commands and see output
- Window title shows current process/CWD
- Process shows as "FrankenTerm" in Activity Monitor with correct icon","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonDune","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:54:43.832455Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T20:45:07.501374Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T20:45:07.501307Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","gui","milestone"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:54:43.832455Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:24.086024Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:24.383853Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1682,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"## Why This Is The Critical Milestone

Until this bead is done, FrankenTerm is functionally just a management CLI. This single bead transforms it into an actual terminal emulator. Every other bead in the epic is gated on this.

Expected difficulty: MEDIUM-HIGH. The main risk is API drift between the modified vendored crates and the unmodified GUI-layer crates. The legacy_wezterm snapshot is from commit 05343b38 (Feb 2026) and the vendored crates have diverged with new types, new codec PDUs, and modified APIs.

Validation approach: get "hello world" working first (just a window with a shell), then progressively enable features. Don't try to activate swap layouts / floating panes / resize scorecards in the initial milestone — that's what .6/.7 are for.","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:00:05Z"},{"id":1696,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Review: Add first-launch experience to acceptance criteria. When user downloads FrankenTerm.app and double-clicks: (1) should open immediately with sensible defaults, (2) should show a brief welcome message or tip about ft CLI integration, (3) should NOT require any config file to exist. The .8 bead handles config but .3 must work with zero config.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:40:00Z"},{"id":1705,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"IMPORTANT: The GUI must start a mux server on a Unix domain socket so that ft CLI commands (ft robot state, ft watch, ft search, etc.) can communicate with it. WezTerm does this automatically at startup. frankenterm-gui must do the same with a FrankenTerm-specific socket path (e.g., /tmp/frankenterm-mux-XXXX.sock or configured via FRANKENTERM_UNIX_SOCKET). Without this, the entire ft CLI integration breaks — ft commands would have no way to reach the running terminal. This is NOT optional for .3 — it's part of 'minimal GUI binary that opens a terminal window' because the mux socket is how the terminal is managed.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:47:03Z"},{"id":1715,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SEQUENCING NOTE: The initial GUI binary (.3) should use the vendored crates AS-IS with their current async runtimes (smol/async-io). The asupersync migration (.13, .14, .15) happens AFTER the GUI is working. This means .3 may temporarily have smol in the dependency tree. That is acceptable — the goal of .3 is 'open a working terminal window', not 'with perfect runtime purity'. The runtime migration is a separate track that runs in parallel.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:08:52Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-1memj.30","title":"[gui][test] Agent swarm stress test suite (200+ panes)","description":"# Agent swarm stress test suite (200+ panes)

## What
Stress tests that validate frankenterm-gui under extreme agent swarm workloads: 200+ panes, high output throughput, rapid layout changes, concurrent input.

## Why
This is FrankenTerm's raison d'etre — being robust under agent swarm load. Stock WezTerm degrades with 50+ panes. FrankenTerm must handle 200+ gracefully.

## Stress Tests

### Pane Scale Tests
- `stress_50_panes_idle` — 50 panes open, idle: memory < 500MB, CPU < 5%
- `stress_100_panes_idle` — 100 panes open, idle: memory < 1GB, CPU < 5%
- `stress_200_panes_idle` — 200 panes open, idle: memory < 2GB, CPU < 10%
- `stress_50_panes_active` — 50 panes with continuous output (1KB/s each): no frame drops
- `stress_200_panes_active` — 200 panes with output: graceful degradation (backpressure kicks in)

### Output Throughput Tests
- `stress_single_pane_10mb` — single pane, 10MB output burst: time to render < 5s
- `stress_single_pane_100mb` — single pane, 100MB output: no OOM, scrollback tiered
- `stress_50_panes_concurrent_output` — 50 panes, 100KB/s each simultaneously

### Input Concurrency Tests
- `stress_concurrent_send_50` — send text to 50 panes simultaneously: no data interleave
- `stress_rapid_pane_create_destroy` — create and destroy 100 panes in 10 seconds: no leak

### Layout Stress Tests
- `stress_rapid_resize_100` — resize window 100 times in 10 seconds: no crash, no artifact
- `stress_layout_cycle_under_load` — cycle layouts while 50 panes have active output
- `stress_float_unfloat_rapid` — float/unfloat panes 100 times: no state corruption

### Resource Monitoring
Each test logs:
```json
{
  "test": "stress_200_panes_idle",
  "pane_count": 200,
  "rss_mb": 1847,
  "cpu_percent": 8.2,
  "frame_time_p50_ms": 12,
  "frame_time_p99_ms": 28,
  "events_dropped": 0,
  "backpressure_tier": "Yellow",
  "duration_s": 30,
  "status": "pass"
}
```

### E2E Script: `scripts/e2e_swarm_stress.sh`
```
1. Launch frankenterm-gui + ft watch
2. Create 50 panes programmatically
3. In each pane: run `yes "pane_N output" | head -10000`
4. Monitor: RSS, CPU, frame time, event throughput
5. Verify: ft watch captured output from all 50 panes (search for "pane_N output")
6. Verify: no pane has 0 bytes captured (all panes observed)
7. Create 150 more panes (total 200)
8. Verify: backpressure activates (tier >= Yellow)
9. Verify: GUI remains responsive (can type in focused pane)
10. Close all panes, verify: memory returns to baseline within 30s
11. Report: peak RSS, peak CPU, p99 frame time, events captured, events dropped
```

## Pass/Fail Criteria
| Metric | 50 panes | 200 panes |
|--------|----------|-----------|
| RSS | < 500MB | < 2GB |
| CPU (idle) | < 5% | < 10% |
| Frame p99 | < 16ms | < 33ms |
| Event loss | 0% | < 1% |
| Responsive | yes | yes (focused pane) |

## Acceptance Criteria
- All stress tests pass with resource limits met
- No OOM, no panics, no hung processes
- Backpressure activates gracefully (not crash)
- Structured logs enable capacity planning","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:37.872527Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:08:33.344254Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gui","performance","stress","swarm","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.30","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:37.872527Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.30","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:54.813718Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.30","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:55.118216Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.30","depends_on_id":"ft-iehgn","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:08:33.344170Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-1memj.31","title":"[gui][test] Config system and auto-detection test suite","description":"# Config system and auto-detection test suite

## What
Tests for the TOML config system (.8), Lua migration, and the ft watch native event auto-detection (.5).

## Why
The config system is a user-facing surface — misparses, bad error messages, or silent config drops cause user frustration. The auto-detection logic is a state machine that needs edge case coverage.

## Config Tests

### TOML Config Parse Tests
- test_config_parse_minimal — minimal frankenterm.toml loads without error
- test_config_parse_full — fully populated config loads all fields
- test_config_parse_invalid_toml — syntax error produces clear message with line number
- test_config_parse_unknown_key — unknown key produces warning (not error)
- test_config_parse_type_mismatch — wrong type produces clear error
- test_config_default_values — unpopulated keys get documented defaults
- test_config_ssh_domains — SSH domain config roundtrip
- test_config_scrollback_tiers — tiered scrollback config roundtrip
- test_config_keybindings — custom keybinding parse and override

### Lua Migration Tests
- test_migrate_config_basic — wezterm.lua with fonts/colors to frankenterm.toml
- test_migrate_config_keybindings — Lua keybindings to TOML keybindings
- test_migrate_config_ssh_domains — Lua SSH domains to TOML
- test_migrate_config_unknown_lua — unknown Lua constructs logged as warnings
- test_migrate_config_idempotent — migrating twice produces same result

### Auto-Detection Tests (ft watch native events)
- test_autodetect_no_socket — no socket falls back to polling
- test_autodetect_socket_present — socket exists connects to push
- test_autodetect_socket_appears_midrun — socket appears while running upgrades to push
- test_autodetect_socket_disappears_midrun — socket removed falls back to polling
- test_autodetect_socket_stale — stale socket stays on polling
- test_autodetect_rapid_toggle — socket appears/disappears rapidly no crash

### E2E Script: scripts/e2e_config.sh
1. Generate a test wezterm.lua with known settings
2. Run ft setup migrate-config --input test.lua --output test.toml
3. Validate generated TOML against expected values
4. Launch frankenterm-gui with generated TOML verify settings applied

 {"id":"ft-1memj.32","title":"[gui][test] Asupersync migration validation suite","description":"# Asupersync migration validation suite

## What
Tests that validate the tokio/smol to asupersync migration is complete and correct across all layers.

## Why
The asupersync migration touches every async boundary in the stack. A missed tokio import or lingering smol dependency causes runtime conflicts (multiple reactors), subtle deadlocks, or panics. We need automated verification.

## Dependency Tree Validation Tests
- test_no_tokio_in_dep_tree — cargo tree -p frankenterm-gui shows no tokio
- test_no_smol_in_dep_tree — cargo tree -p frankenterm-gui shows no smol
- test_no_async_io_in_dep_tree — cargo tree -p frankenterm-gui shows no async-io
- test_no_tokio_imports_in_source — grep -r 'tokio::' across all frankenterm crates returns 0 matches
- test_no_smol_imports_in_source — grep -r 'smol::' across all frankenterm crates returns 0 matches

## Runtime Validation Tests (require asupersync runtime)
- test_compat_runtime_is_asupersync — CompatRuntime::current() reports asupersync backend
- test_spawn_task_runs — spawn a task that sets an AtomicBool, verify it ran
- test_channel_send_recv — asupersync channel send/recv works
- test_sleep_wakes — asupersync::sleep returns after duration
- test_concurrent_io_100_fds — 100 concurrent fd read/write operations
- test_tcp_listen_connect — TCP listener + client connect via asupersync
- test_unix_socket_roundtrip — UDS send/recv (critical for native events)

## PTY Migration Tests
- test_pty_on_asupersync — open PTY, read/write via asupersync reactor
- test_pty_100_concurrent_asupersync — 100 concurrent PTYs on asupersync
- test_pty_no_smol_fallback — verify smol is not loaded at runtime

## Window Event Loop Tests (macOS)
- test_event_loop_asupersync_integration — window events processed via asupersync
- test_event_loop_no_smol_tasks — verify no smol task spawning during window lifecycle

## Performance Comparison
- bench_pty_latency_asupersync — PTY read/write latency (baseline comparison)
- bench_channel_throughput_asupersync — channel messages per second
- bench_spawn_overhead_asupersync — task spawn cost (should be < 1 microsecond)

## E2E Script: scripts/e2e_asupersync.sh
1. cargo tree -p frankenterm-gui -- check zero tokio/smol
2. Build and launch frankenterm-gui
3. Open shell, type commands, verify PTY I/O works
4. Start ft watch, verify native event socket works
5. Open SSH domain (if configured), verify SSH I/O works
6. Run pty latency benchmark, compare against baseline
7. Report: pass/fail per validation, latency numbers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero tokio/smol/async-io in dependency tree
- All runtime tests pass on asupersync
- No performance regression vs tokio baseline (within 10%)
- PTY, mux, network all work on asupersync","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:14.052442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T21:00:00.885936Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T21:00:00.885936Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","gui","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.32","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:14.052442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.32","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:18.785892Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.32","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:19.162165Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.32","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:19.533220Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.32","depends_on_id":"ft-2ojrv","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:16.445874Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1904,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.32","author":"RusticHeron","text":"Taking a bounded support slice against this blocked bead rather than force-claiming it. Validation is running via rch with CARGO_TARGET_DIR moved to /Volumes/USB_NVME because the system data volume is near full and local fallback previously failed with os error 28.","created_at":"2026-03-05T20:50:43Z"},{"id":1905,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.32","author":"RusticHeron","text":"Implemented support slice in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/runtime_compat_smoke.rs: replaced the detached-task smoke check with deterministic std::sync::mpsc signaling; added backend-agnostic runtime_compat smoke coverage for mpsc round-trip, watch change consumption via runtime_compat helpers, and timeout error surfacing for a slow future.  {"id":"ft-1memj.33","title":"[gui][mux] Headless mux server binary for remote agent fleets","description":"# Headless mux server binary for remote agent fleets

## What
Build a frankenterm-mux-server binary that runs the FrankenTerm mux without a GUI — enabling agent fleets on headless servers.

## Why
The GUI binary (frankenterm-gui) requires a display. But agent swarms on remote servers (CI/CD, cloud VMs, Mac Minis) need FrankenTerm's mux capabilities without a screen. WezTerm solves this with a separate wezterm-mux-server binary. FrankenTerm needs the same.

Without this, remote agent fleets must either: (1) use stock WezTerm's mux server (losing all FrankenTerm features), or (2) run with X forwarding (impractical at scale).

## Use Cases
1. Remote server runs frankenterm-mux-server, operator connects from local frankenterm-gui via --connect host:port
2. CI/CD pipeline spawns frankenterm-mux-server to manage agent sessions, ft CLI commands manage them
3. Mac Mini build farm runs headless frankenterm-mux-server for agent orchestration
4. ft watch connects to local mux server for event monitoring without a GUI

## How
1. Create crates/frankenterm-mux-server/ with a minimal main.rs that starts the mux domain listener
2. Reuse the vendored mux/pty/term crates (same as GUI, minus window/font/rendering)
3. Listen on configurable Unix socket and/or TCP port
4. Support TLS for network connections (reuse distributed mode's TLS config)
5. Start native event socket for ft watch integration

## Key Differences from GUI
- No window crate dependency (no rendering, no GPU)
- No font crate dependency
- Much smaller binary (target: <20 MB vs ~135 MB for GUI)
- Can run as systemd service or launchd agent
- Config: frankenterm.toml (same as GUI, rendering keys ignored)

## Tests
- test_mux_server_starts_socket — starts and creates socket file
- test_mux_server_accepts_client — frankenterm-gui client can connect
- test_mux_server_creates_pane — remote pane creation works
- test_mux_server_pty_io — type in remote pane, see output
- test_mux_server_native_events — ft watch receives events from headless server
- test_mux_server_graceful_shutdown — SIGTERM cleanly stops server
- test_mux_server_tls — TLS connection works with cert/key pair

## E2E Script: scripts/e2e_mux_server.sh
1. Start frankenterm-mux-server on localhost
2. Verify socket created
3. Run ft robot state against the mux server
4. Create a pane via ft robot (or mux protocol)
5. Send echo hello via ft robot send
6. Verify output captured via ft search
7. Start ft watch, verify native events flow
8. SIGTERM the server, verify clean shutdown
9. Report: pass/fail per step with timing

## Acceptance Criteria
- frankenterm-mux-server runs headlessly (no display required)
- ft CLI commands work against it (ft robot state, ft watch, etc.)
- Remote GUI connection works (frankenterm-gui --connect localhost:port)
- Binary size < 20 MB
- Native event socket works for ft watch integration
- Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:00:58.091633Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-23T01:20:01.905465Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T01:20:01.905123Z","close_reason":"Validated existing headless mux server implementation and fixed final mux-server clippy warning","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gui","headless","mux","remote"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.33","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:00:58.091633Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.33","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:03:07.926504Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.33","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.20","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:01:05.847507Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.33","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.24","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:01:06.260915Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1714,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.33","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DEPENDENCY NOTE: Depends on .1 (workspace setup) NOT .2 (vendor window/font). The mux server specifically excludes window and font crates — it only needs mux, pty, term, config, codec which are ALREADY vendored and in the workspace. This means .33 can be built as soon as .1 completes, in parallel with .2 and .3.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:03:14Z"},{"id":1728,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.33","author":"LavenderFox","text":"Implemented headless mux server crate slice: added crates/frankenterm-mux-server (binary) and crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl (listener/session dispatch helper), adapted from in-repo legacy_wezterm source; wired workspace membership/dependency. Also aligned command/default naming to frankenterm-mux-server in config serve command and ft mux restart/systemd setup paths. Validation attempted via rch only, but currently blocked by remote fleet connectivity (rch workers probe --all reports all workers unreachable); aborted local fallbacks to stay offload-only compliant.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:22:40Z"},{"id":1729,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.33","author":"LavenderFox","text":"Added scripts/e2e_mux_server.sh smoke harness (timed pass/fail steps for start/socket/state/send+search/events/SIGTERM, with --dry-run and env overrides). bash -n passes locally. Still blocked on rch remote compile verification because all workers currently unreachable.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:24:45Z"},{"id":1730,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.33","author":"LavenderFox","text":"Validated scripts/e2e_mux_server.sh control flow: bash -n passes; --dry-run now reports pass=6/fail=0 after bypassing binary presence checks in dry-run mode.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:26:30Z"},{"id":2473,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.33","author":"Codex","text":"Revalidation 2026-03-23:

Fresh validation:
- `bash -n scripts/e2e_mux_server.sh` => pass
- `scripts/e2e_mux_server.sh --dry-run` => pass (pass=6 fail=0 skip=4 total=10)
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/home/ubuntu CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/ubuntu/ft-1memj-33-check cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server --bin frankenterm-mux-server` => `[RCH] remote vmi1264463` pass

Fresh-eyes bug found during remote clippy validation:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/home/ubuntu CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/ubuntu/ft-1memj-33-clippy cargo clippy -p frankenterm-mux-server --bin frankenterm-mux-server --no-deps -- -D warnings` surfaced a real warning in `crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/ossl.rs:130` (`clippy::needless_borrows_for_generic_args`)
- Fixed by changing `set_certificate_chain_file(&chain_file)` to `set_certificate_chain_file(chain_file)`
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/ossl.rs` => pass
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/home/ubuntu CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/ubuntu/ft-1memj-33-clippy-fix cargo clippy -p frankenterm-mux-server --bin frankenterm-mux-server --no-deps -- -D warnings` => `[RCH] remote vmi1264463` pass

No additional acceptance gaps surfaced in this lane after the fix + revalidation.","created_at":"2026-03-23T01:20:01Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-iehgn","title":"[EPIC] Ultra-Performance FrankenTerm for Massive Agent Swarms (200+ panes)","description":"# Ultra-Performance FrankenTerm for Massive Agent Swarms

## Vision
FrankenTerm managing 200+ concurrent AI agent panes on a 512GB RAM / 64-core server with zero degradation. Every subsystem optimized to handle the unique load profile of agent swarms: bursty output, long-lived sessions, competing builds, and cascading resource exhaustion.

## Methodology — MANDATORY SKILLS
Each implementation bead under this epic MUST invoke the appropriate skill:

1. **/extreme-software-optimization** — Profile-driven optimization with behavior proofs. Use for ALL performance work. Never optimize blind — measure first, prove the fix, measure after.
2. **/alien-artifact-coding** — Elicit deep mathematical knowledge for formal guarantees. Use for lock-free data structures, scheduling algorithms, and any code where correctness under concurrency is critical.
3. **/idea-wizard** — Generate and operationalize improvement ideas. Use when brainstorming novel approaches to scaling problems.

## Load Profile (200+ agent panes)
- **Processes**: 600-2000+ (agents + MCP servers + child builds/tests)
- **File descriptors**: 5000-15000+ (pipes, sockets, files per pane)
- **Memory**: 60-200GB baseline (300MB-1GB per agent)
- **CPU**: Bursty — idle agents near 0%, active builds at 100% per core
- **I/O**: Mixed — agents reading/writing files, competing cargo builds thrashing disk
- **Network**: Moderate — MCP, HTTP, Unix socket IPC

## Key Insight from /system-performance-remediation
The #1 cause of swarm performance meltdowns is NOT individual pane overhead — it's **cascading resource exhaustion**: one stuck agent spawns competing builds, which starve other agents of CPU, which causes them to timeout and retry, spawning MORE builds. The fix is proactive lifecycle management, not faster I/O.

## Architecture Principles
1. **Pressure-aware**: Monitor /proc/pressure/cpu,memory,io and react BEFORE meltdown
2. **Lifecycle-managed**: Every pane has age/activity tracking with automatic cleanup policies
3. **Coordinated**: Agents share build caches, coordinate resource usage via FrankenTerm
4. **Formally verified**: Critical data structures proven correct under concurrency (/alien-artifact-coding)
5. **Profile-driven**: Never optimize without profiling first (/extreme-software-optimization)

## Success Criteria
1. The complete epic scope is delivered across child beads with no unresolved critical blockers and no loss of declared capability.
2. Every child feature/task in this epic includes comprehensive unit tests plus deterministic integration/E2E scenarios.
3. The epic includes an end-to-end validation matrix covering happy paths, failure injection, recovery behavior, and sustained/soak execution where applicable.
4. Structured logging and artifact standards are enforced across epic deliverables (timestamp, correlation_id, scenario_id, decision path, reason_code/error_code) with triage-ready evidence bundles.
5. Verification demonstrates stable, repeatable outcomes across reruns and confirms policy/safety invariants for all touched subsystems.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Ultra-performance epic must be validated by explicit unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end scripts, benchmark suites, and soak runs against swarm-scale workloads.
2. Performance claims require measurable before/after evidence (latency/throughput/memory/cpu) with reproducible harness settings and regression thresholds.
3. Structured diagnostics/logging and artifact bundles are mandatory for all optimization experiments and gate decisions.
4. No optimization ships without correctness/non-regression evidence for user-visible behavior and failure-path handling.
5. Closure requires evidence-backed sign-off with residual risk ownership and rollback readiness.","notes":"Keep optimization work proof-driven and user-outcome oriented; reject speculative changes that do not carry unit/integration/e2e evidence and reproducible benchmark artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:10:28.451043Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-10T05:43:50.372029Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-iehgn","depends_on_id":"ft-1sm78","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-iehgn","depends_on_id":"ft-1ta8j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-iehgn","depends_on_id":"ft-27hy6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-iehgn","depends_on_id":"ft-2ahu0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-iehgn","depends_on_id":"ft-2oph2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-iehgn","depends_on_id":"ft-8eoug","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-iehgn","depends_on_id":"ft-9i3rh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":945,"issue_id":"ft-iehgn","author":"BronzeReef","text":"Progress update (BronzeReef, 2026-02-18):

 {"id":"ft-iehgn.1","title":"Wire Backpressure Tier Into Runtime Health Snapshot","description":"Goal: surface concrete backpressure tier (GREEN/YELLOW/RED/BLACK) in runtime health snapshots so 200+ pane operators can triage saturation before loss.

Scope:
- Use existing queue depth signals (capture/write) and backpressure classifier to populate HealthSnapshot.backpressure_tier.
- Keep behavior deterministic and backward-compatible for consumers that treat the field as optional.
- Add/adjust focused tests in runtime.rs for tier presence and serialization behavior.

Acceptance:
- Health snapshots from runtime include non-null backpressure_tier when runtime is running.
- Existing warning signals remain intact.

 {"id":"ft-iehgn.2","title":"Eliminate per-snapshot BackpressureManager allocation in health tier classification","description":"Goal: optimize runtime health snapshot backpressure-tier classification by removing BackpressureManager construction on every call while preserving exact tier semantics.\n\nScope:\n- Replace helper implementation in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs with a pure, zero-allocation classifier that mirrors BackpressureManager::classify thresholds and saturation behavior.\n- Keep existing optional behavior: return None when both capacities are unknown (0/0).\n- Add focused tests proving helper equivalence to BackpressureManager::classify across representative depth/capacity combinations.\n\nAcceptance:\n- Helper no longer instantiates BackpressureManager in hot path.\n- Classification output remains behaviorally equivalent for known capacities.\n- Targeted runtime tests pass via rch exec.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzeReef","created_at":"2026-02-18T05:38:42.877726Z","created_by":"BronzeReef","updated_at":"2026-02-18T05:44:30.288599Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T05:44:30.288577Z","close_reason":"Completed zero-allocation runtime classifier refactor with equivalence tests; focused validations pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-iehgn.2","depends_on_id":"ft-iehgn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":947,"issue_id":"ft-iehgn.2","author":"BronzeReef","text":"Completed `ft-iehgn.2` runtime classifier optimization in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs`.

Delivered scope:
- Reworked `classify_backpressure_tier` to a pure, zero-allocation classification path (no per-call `BackpressureManager` construction).
- Preserved existing optional semantics: returns `None` when both capacities are unknown (`0/0`).
- Preserved tier behavior by mirroring `BackpressureManager::classify` saturation + threshold rules (`BLACK` saturation guardrails first, then `RED`, `YELLOW`, `GREEN`).
- Added equivalence coverage:
  - `classify_backpressure_tier_matches_manager_semantics` compares helper output to `BackpressureManager::classify` across a matrix of capacities/depths.

Validation via `rch exec`:
- Focused tests: `cargo test -p frankenterm-core classify_backpressure_tier --lib` ✅ (3 passed).
- Targeted compile: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib` ✅.
- Required workspace gates:
  - `cargo fmt --check` ❌ pre-existing formatting drift across many unrelated files.
  - `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ❌ pre-existing unrelated compile failures in `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` (`agent_correlator` inventory symbols).
  - `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ❌ pre-existing unrelated lint debt across other modules/crates.

@@ -69,6 +69,7 @@ Store API in `mmap_store.rs` includes:
 - `tests/e2e/test_ft_8vla_mmap_scrollback.sh`
 - Emits structured JSONL logs (`tests/e2e/logs/ft_8vla_mmap_scrollback_*.jsonl`) with nominal, failure-injection, recovery, and benchmark-compile checks.
 - Executes cargo workloads through `rch exec -- ...` and now fails closed if workers are unreachable or `rch` falls back to local execution.
+- Uses a repo-relative `target/rch-e2e-ft-8vla-<run_id>` target dir for repeated remote legs so `rch` can keep the same artifact tree stable across steps; this avoids the `/tmp`-scoped dep-info churn that can break later remote `cargo test` invocations.
 - Uses `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --bench mmap_scrollback --message-format short` for the benchmark compile contract. This matches the repo's other bench guards, stays on `rch`'s remote-only path, and avoids the unstable full remote bench-link path that was failing independently of the mmap logic.
 - On macOS, forces `TMPDIR=/tmp` for `rch` invocations to avoid ControlMaster socket path-length failures from masquerading as remote-offload success.

@@ -10,7 +10,13 @@ SCENARIO_ID="ft_8vla_mmap_scrollback"

 STDOUT_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/${SCENARIO_ID}_${RUN_ID}.stdout.log"
-TARGET_DIR="${CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-/tmp/target-rch-ft-8vla}-${RUN_ID}"
+DEFAULT_CARGO_TARGET_DIR="target/rch-e2e-ft-8vla-${RUN_ID}"
+INHERITED_CARGO_TARGET_DIR="${CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-}"

+  TARGET_DIR="${INHERITED_CARGO_TARGET_DIR}"
+else
+  TARGET_DIR="${DEFAULT_CARGO_TARGET_DIR}"
+fi
 LAST_STEP_LOG=""
 RCH_FAIL_OPEN_REGEX='\[RCH\][[:space:]]+local|Remote execution failed: .*running locally|Failed to connect to ubuntu@|too long for Unix domain socket'
 RCH_SOCKET_PATH_REGEX='unix_listener: path .*too long for Unix domain socket|too long for Unix domain socket'
